project.name: AI-native personal PM system
project.context: Built on top of existing sprint platform (Supabase + GitHub Pages). Extension of the OpenClaw bridge concept previously designed. Solves the scattered-projects problem across Claude conversations, HTML tools, and meetings.

problem.core: Multiple active projects scattered across Claude conversations with no unified view, no persistent memory across sessions, and no way to query semantically across all content.
pain_points: Claude conversations end and context is lost
HTML tools (sprint board, change management suite) produce outputs that go nowhere
Meeting notes stay in notebooks or voice memos, never connected to projects
No portfolio view — impossible to see all projects at a glance
Status is always in your head, never in a system

previous_attempts.name: GitHub JSON dashboard
previous_attempts.what_worked: Read layer was clean — dashboard fetched JSON, rendered well
previous_attempts.what_failed: Write path required manual JSON editing in GitHub. Drifted immediately. No intelligence.

previous_attempts.name: OpenClaw bridge (v1 concept)
previous_attempts.what_worked: Correct architectural instinct — local agent as intermediary
previous_attempts.what_failed: Missing the intelligence layer. No structured extraction. No vector layer. Never built.

core_concept: A local-first, AI-native project operating system. Raw inputs flow into an orchestration layer on a Mac Mini. Two local models run in parallel on every input: Gemma 3 4B extracts structured data, nomic-embed-text generates a semantic vector. Both write to Supabase. A static GitHub Pages dashboard reads from Supabase for exact queries and semantic search. Zero cloud inference cost. Private data stays local.
principles: Capture friction must be near zero — every input path under 60 seconds
Intelligence at ingestion, not at query time
Two query modes always available: exact SQL and semantic similarity
No cloud dependency for AI — everything runs on Mac Mini
Static frontend — no backend server to maintain beyond OpenClaw

layer_1_inputs.description: Three ways to get data into the system. All converge on OpenClaw POST /ingest.

sources.name: update.html
sources.flow: Open on phone or desktop → paste raw text (meeting notes, brain dump, Claude export) → POST to OpenClaw via tunnel → confirmation toast

sources.name: Claude conversation export
sources.flow: At end of any Claude conversation → ask Claude to summarise as project update → copy output → paste into update.html OR drop as .txt into watched folder
sources.note: Claude.ai CSP blocks direct API calls from chat. Copy-paste is the bridge.

sources.name: File watcher — ~/pm-inbox/
sources.flow: Drop any .txt file into watched folder on Mac Mini → OpenClaw detects new file → auto-ingests → file moved to ~/pm-inbox/processed/

layer_2_orchestration.description: OpenClaw HTTP server running on Mac Mini. The intelligence layer. Receives raw text, runs two parallel AI jobs, writes structured + vector data to Supabase.

endpoints.input: { text: string, project_hint?: string, source?: string }
endpoints.output: { success: true, project_id, update_id, tasks_created, decisions_created }

endpoints.input: { query: string, limit?: number, project_id?: string }
endpoints.output: top-k semantically similar chunks with source metadata

endpoints.description: Proxy list of projects from Supabase — useful for update.html dropdown
Returns Ollama status, Supabase connectivity, last ingest timestamp

jobs.name: classify_input
jobs.prompt_goal: Determine what type of input this is and which project it belongs to

jobs.name: extract_structure
jobs.prompt_goal: Pull structured data from raw text — tasks, decisions, status changes, key points

jobs.name: generate_embedding
jobs.note: Runs in parallel with Gemma jobs — not sequential. Both fire at same time.

layer_3_remote_access.description: Makes OpenClaw reachable from outside home network so update.html on GitHub Pages works from phone, client site, anywhere.

options.name: Tailscale
options.why: Zero config, stable, private mesh VPN, free for personal use. Mac Mini gets a permanent TS IP. update.html POSTs to that IP.

options.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
options.why: Public HTTPS URL without exposing home IP. Better if you want to share update.html with others.
options.tradeoff: Slightly more config, but public URL is a security consideration

layer_4_storage.setup_command: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

tables.name: projects
tables.columns: id (uuid), code (text), title (text), status (text), description (text), context_url (text), facilitator (text), updated_at (timestamptz), created_at (timestamptz)
tables.purpose: Master project registry. One row per project. Status: active | paused | done | archived.

tables.name: updates
tables.columns: id (uuid), project_id (fk), raw_text (text), summary (text), input_type (text), source (text), submitted_at (timestamptz)
tables.purpose: Timestamped log of everything that happened. Raw + structured. The append-only event log.

tables.name: tasks
tables.columns: id (uuid), project_id (fk), update_id (fk nullable), title (text), status (text), due_date (date nullable), created_at (timestamptz)
tables.purpose: Extracted tasks from updates. Can be created manually or auto-extracted by Gemma.

tables.name: decisions
tables.columns: id (uuid), project_id (fk), update_id (fk nullable), body (text), made_at (timestamptz)
tables.purpose: Explicit decisions captured. Queryable separately from general updates.

tables.name: embeddings
tables.columns: id (uuid), source_id (uuid), source_type (text), content (text), embedding (vector(768)), created_at (timestamptz)
tables.purpose: Semantic memory layer. Every update, decision and task gets a vector. Enables natural language search across all project history.
tables.index: CREATE INDEX ON embeddings USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops);

query_modes.exact_sql: Filter by project, status, date range, input_type. Standard Supabase REST queries from dashboard.
query_modes.semantic_search: POST /search → OpenClaw embeds query → pgvector cosine similarity → returns top-k chunks with source metadata → display in search.html

layer_5_ui.hosting: GitHub Pages — same repo as sprint platform or new repo

pages.reads: Supabase REST — projects, recent updates, open tasks

pages.writes: POST to OpenClaw /ingest via Tailscale URL
features: Submit → shows extracted summary for confirmation
Each result shows source project, date, type
Click → opens full update in context

hardware.note: 8GB unified memory comfortably fits Gemma 3 4B + nomic-embed-text simultaneously

orchestration.name: OpenClaw
orchestration.file_watcher: chokidar (Node) or watchdog (Python) depending on OpenClaw language

database.name: Supabase
database.plan: Free tier — sufficient for personal use
database.existing_project: Same project as sprint platform (reuse connection)

tunnel.name: Tailscale (recommended)
tunnel.purpose: Expose OpenClaw to internet for update.html

frontend.hosting: GitHub Pages — blackmath88/onehour-minisprint or new repo
frontend.design_system: Existing — Plus Jakarta Sans + Indigo-on-Cream palette

tech_stack.estimated_monthly_cost: $0 — all free tiers, zero cloud inference

build_sequence.name: Foundation
tasks: Install Ollama on Mac Mini if not present
ollama pull gemma3:4b
ollama pull nomic-embed-text
Run CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector in Supabase SQL editor
Create all five tables (projects, updates, tasks, decisions, embeddings)
Seed 3-5 real active projects manually
Verify Supabase REST queries work from browser
build_sequence.done_when: Dashboard can read projects from Supabase

build_sequence.name: Ingestion pipeline
tasks: Add POST /ingest route to OpenClaw
Call Ollama gemma3:4b for classify_input job
Call Ollama gemma3:4b for extract_structure job
Call Ollama nomic-embed-text for embedding job
Write structured rows to Supabase tables
Write embedding row to embeddings table
Test with curl from terminal
build_sequence.done_when: curl POST with raw text → structured row appears in Supabase → embedding stored

build_sequence.name: Remote access
tasks: Install Tailscale on Mac Mini
Note the Tailscale IP for OpenClaw port
Update update.html to POST to Tailscale URL
Test POST from mobile browser
build_sequence.done_when: update.html on GitHub Pages POSTs successfully to Mac Mini from 4G

build_sequence.name: Dashboard + capture UI
tasks: Build index.html — project cards, recent activity, open tasks
Build update.html — project dropdown, textarea, POST to /ingest
Add confirmation step showing Gemma-extracted summary before save
Wire search bar to POST /search → display results
build_sequence.done_when: Full loop: paste note on phone → appears in dashboard → searchable

build_sequence.name: File watcher
tasks: Add file watcher to OpenClaw for ~/pm-inbox/ folder
Auto-ingest any new .txt file
Move processed files to ~/pm-inbox/processed/
Test by dropping a Claude conversation export into the folder
build_sequence.done_when: Drop text file → appears in dashboard automatically

open_questions.question: What language and framework is OpenClaw running on?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Determines whether new routes are Node.js, Python, or something else

open_questions.question: Is Ollama already installed on the Mac Mini?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Phase 1 dependency — if not, install first

open_questions.question: Tailscale or Cloudflare Tunnel preference?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Tailscale is simpler for personal use, Cloudflare gives a public URL

open_questions.question: What are the 3-5 active projects to seed first?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Shapes the schema validation and makes phase 1 immediately useful

open_questions.question: Should this live in the existing sprint platform repo or a new one?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Determines file structure and whether supabase-client.js is shared

explicitly_deferred: Automatic Claude conversation capture (blocked by Claude.ai CSP)
Cloud inference fallback for when Mac Mini is offline

connection_to_existing_work.sprint_platform: Same Supabase project, same design system, same GitHub Pages repo. Sessions from sprint board can be linked to projects via project_id.
connection_to_existing_work.imd_change_tool: Change project phases map directly to updates table. Tool outputs become ingestion candidates.
connection_to_existing_work.design_sprint_kb: Knowledge base content can be vectorized and added to embeddings — enables semantic search across methodology + project data together.
